dc_abstract: Photograph of the A&M Kountry Store on Armstrong's Day, the store features advertisements for Emerald-Oil and Cooklite. A shows pavilion has ovens provided by Texas Power & Light Company and tables from East Side Furniture Company on display.
dc_description: Held in the Bryan Scenes picture file at Cushing Memorial Library on the campus of Texas A&M University, College Station, Texas.

dc_abstract: Photography of the J.W. Howell residence which was located at the intersection of what is now 29th Street and Texas Avenue. The Howell house was razed in the 1960s to make room for the City National Bank building.  Otis Instruments occupies the site today.
dc_description: Held in the Bryan Scenes picture file at Cushing Memorial Library on the campus of Texas A&M University, College Station, Texas.

dc_abstract: The Opera House, later called the Palace Theater, was built in 1910. The theater's roof collapsed in 1987, in the early 1990s the Lyle Loveet used the stage as the setting for his music video to "This Old Porch." The city renovated the site in the late 1990s to become the outdoor venue it is today.
dc_description: Held in the Bryan Scenes picture file at Cushing Memorial Library on the campus of Texas A&M University, College Station, Texas.
